title: Ordered CatBoost Specialist On Raw High-Cardinality Race Context
summary: CatBoost’s ordered categorical statistics are designed to reduce target leakage, and high-cardinality benchmarks favor regularized target encoding over naive integer/one-hot schemes.
rationale: CatBoost’s ordered categorical statistics are designed to reduce target leakage, and high-cardinality benchmarks favor regularized target encoding over naive integer/one-hot schemes. This fits the dataset’s many rare `Driver` identities and interaction-heavy context (`Driver`,`Race`,`Year`,`Compound`,`Stint`) better than more manual frequency features.
implementation_hint: Train one specialist with raw categoricals plus a few explicit combos such as `Driver x Race_Year`, `Driver x Compound`, `Race_Year x LapBucket`, `Compound x Stint`. Validate with grouped folds by `Race_Year` and keep row order chronological within each race/driver so ordered CTRs stay causal. Compare standalone score and rank-blend with the current tree model.
expected_effect: Should add complementary signal on sparse driver- and event-specific pit behavior, especially where manual heuristics flatten rare categories.
risk: If folds are not grouped chronologically, ordered CTR gains can be illusory; with strong race holdout, some combos may be too sparse and collapse to priors.
